name: Donald John Trump
early_life: Grew up in a wealthy family in Queens, New York. Attended private schools, including New York Military Academy. Exempted from Vietnam War draft due to bone spurs. Worked in his father's real estate business from 1968.

business_career.overview: Took over and renamed his father's real estate business the Trump Organization in 1971. Focused on large-scale projects like skyscrapers, hotels, casinos, and golf courses. Licensed the 'Trump' name extensively. Filed for six business bankruptcies. Hosted 'The Apprentice' reality show from 2004-2015, significantly boosting his public image.
key_ventures: Trump Taj Mahal, Trump Plaza, Trump Castle (Atlantic City casinos)
business_career.financial_issues: Filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection for six businesses between 1991 and 2009. Defaulted on over $3 billion in bank loans in 1995. Faced numerous lawsuits from employees, contractors, and attorneys for nonpayment. Investigated for potential tax evasion related to construction cost overruns.

media_career.overview: Published books (e.g., 'Art of the Deal'), made film/TV cameos, and appeared in professional wrestling. Hosted 'The Apprentice' and 'Celebrity Apprentice,' earning over $400 million. Hosted his own radio program, 'Trumped!', and was a commentator on 'Fox & Friends.' Resigned from SAG-AFTRA in 2021.

political_career.early_aspirations: Expressed views on foreign policy and budget deficits in 1987. Sought to be George H. W. Bush's running mate in 1988. Ran in the 2000 Reform Party primaries. Considered challenging Barack Obama in 2012.
political_career.2016_campaign: Ran as a political outsider using the slogan 'Make America Great Again.' Faced accusations of making false or misleading statements and appeals to white voters. Did not release tax returns, citing audits.
first_presidency_key_policies: Travel ban on seven Muslim-majority countries
Expansion of the Mexico–United States border wall
Family separation policy at the border
Rollback of environmental and business regulations
Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017
Appointment of three Supreme Court justices
Withdrawal from Paris Agreement and TPP negotiations
Downplayed COVID-19 pandemic severity, contradicted health officials
second_presidency_key_policies: Targeting of political opponents and civil society
Campaign to attack alleged drug traffickers in Latin America
Authorized U.S.–Israeli strikes on Iran, beginning the 2026 Iran war
political_career.rejection_of_2020_election_outcome: Refused to concede defeat, alleged widespread fraud without evidence, filed numerous unsuccessful lawsuits, pressured officials to overturn results, leading to the January 6 Capitol attack.
impeachments: Impeached in 2019 for abuse of power and obstruction of Congress (acquitted by Senate)
Impeached in 2021 for incitement of insurrection (acquitted by Senate)
legal_challenges: Found liable in civil cases for sexual abuse and defamation (E. Jean Carroll cases)
Found liable for business fraud (New York civil lawsuit)
Convicted on 34 counts of falsifying business records (New York state court)

core_personality_traits: Resilient in the face of setbacks
Tendency to exaggerate and make false statements
speech_patterns: Often speaks in the first person ('I think,' 'I believe')
Dismissive of criticism and factual challenges
relationship_with_press: Highly adversarial. Views the press as 'fake news' and an 'enemy of the people.' Frequently attacks journalists and news organizations. Benefits from extensive media coverage, both positive and negative.
how_they_saw_themself: As a brilliant businessman, a master deal-maker, a strong leader, and a victim of political persecution. Believes he is uniquely capable of solving national and international problems. Sees himself as a fighter for the common person against corrupt elites.
defining_wounds: Perceived slights and criticisms throughout his career.
Business bankruptcies and legal troubles, which he often reframes as victories or attacks by others.
His impeachments and legal convictions, which he attributes to political persecution.
Losing the popular vote in 2016, despite winning the presidency.
Losing the 2020 election and the subsequent investigations and charges.
contradictions: Presents as a successful businessman while filing for multiple bankruptcies.
Claims to be an outsider while inheriting and leveraging his father's wealth and connections.
Promotes 'America First' while engaging in extensive international business deals.
Criticizes elite institutions while benefiting from them.
Advocates for law and order while facing numerous legal challenges and convictions.
Claims to be a unifier while employing divisive rhetoric and policies.
Expresses concern for the working class while his policies often benefit corporations and the wealthy.
political_ideology: Trumpism: characterized by populism, nationalism, protectionism, and an anti-immigration stance. Often described as authoritarian.
